Summary: MyoD1 is an important transcription factor and co‑activator of genes responsible for myocyte differentiation and is described as the "master myogenic switch" for cardiac and skeletal muscle development. Wnt signaling suppresses Mohawk (MKX), which is a direct negative regulator of MyoD1 transcription. The authors hypothesize that in TAFAZZIN‑knockout (TAZ‑KO) cells, upregulation of Mohawk (MKX) inhibits MyoD1 transcription. MyoD1 expression is described as dependent on noncanonical β‑catenin‑independent Wnt signaling. β‑catenin levels are not significantly different between WT and TAZ‑KO cells, implicating the noncanonical Wnt pathway in dysfunctional MKX–MyoD1 regulation. Consequently, differentiation of TAZ‑KO myoblasts is significantly reduced in response to differentiation induction. The authors propose that decreased MyoD1 in TAFAZZIN‑deficient myoblasts may underlie skeletal myopathy in BTHS patients.
Reference: Vo, L., Schmidtke, M. W., Da Rosa-Junior, N. T., Ren, M., Schlame, M., & Greenberg, M. L. (2023). Cardiolipin metabolism regulates expression of muscle transcription factor MyoD1 and muscle development. J Biol Chem, 299(3), 102978. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jbc.2023.102978

Summary: Context: BC3H-1 cells, which lacked endogenous MyoD1 mRNA, were transfected with MyoD1 under the beta-actin promoter. Mechanism: the introduced MyoD1 protein localized to the nucleus. Outcome: BD (MyoD1-expressing) lines formed long, multinucleated myotubes instead of the predominantly mononucleated differentiated MHC‑expressing cells seen in parental BC3H‑1, yet BD myotubes and parental BC3H‑1 cells expressed the same pattern of sarcomeric MHC isoforms.

Summary: In embryonic myogenesis, MYOD1 is expressed when PAX3+ muscle precursor cells commit into myoblasts. MYOD1 is described as a marker of late muscle progenitors and is mostly expressed at embryonic day 11.5 (E11.5), indicating the transition to more differentiated muscle cell states. As one of the myogenic regulatory factors, MYOD1 activates muscle differentiation by binding E-box motifs in promoters of muscle-specific genes. This activation precedes further differentiation into MYOG-expressing myocytes. The MYOG-expressing myocytes fuse into myotubes that mature into myofibers. In embryonic myocyte populations, MEF2A activates Mymx expression and RXRG represses Mymx expression, likely through interactions with MYOD1 and MYOG.
